title: reconnect_sse returns tuple; out-params should be &mut
description: reconnect_sse() takes 6 parameters and returns (JoinHandle, UnboundedReceiver). The tuple return means the call site must unpack and reassign both legs: sse_handle = new_handle; sse_rx = new_rx. If a future caller forgets to reassign one leg, the old handle leaks (abort is never called on subsequent disconnects) or sse_rx continues draining the dead channel — both silent misbehaviours. The client/event_source_url/tx/ctx quartet is loop-level context, not output; they should stay as parameters. The actual outputs (handle and rx) should be passed as &mut parameters so the compiler enforces that both are updated. Suggested signature: async fn reconnect_sse(handle: &mut JoinHandle<()>, sse_rx: &mut UnboundedReceiver<SseNotification>, backoff_idx: &mut usize, client: Arc<JmapChatClient>, event_source_url: &str, tx: &EventSender, ctx: &egui::Context). The call site becomes a single statement with no unpacking.
close_reason: Opinion: tuple return is clean because old handle is consumed inside the function; &mut out-params would be more verbose with no clarity gain
